Patch Set 7:

Could you please review this patch? Thank you.

Patch Set 7:

ping

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

What about just setting the lock around tgt_client_data_write() inside the tgt_client_data_update(). This would be much simpler approach, are there any drawbacks with that?

Patch Set 7:

ted_lcd_lock is used to protect ted_lcd structure while it is updating in tgt_client_del()

Patch Set 7:

yes, and we can keep only memset under lock there and add lock around tgt_client_data_write() inside the tgt_client_data_update(). It is still simple and clear. There is no need to have atomic lcd update with lcd write there. Strictly speaking I don't think we have to protect uuid zeroing with lock at all, I can't imagine any race with that which can be serious.

Patch Set 7:

Sorry, I don't understand what you mean.
ted->ted_lcd_lock is acquired in tgt_client_del(), before call of
tgt_client_data_update(). That is before my changes.

Patch Set 7:

I mean that we can just move mutex lock/unlock inside tgt_client_data_update(), around tgt_client_data_write().

commit.subject: LU-7593 target: umount vs tgt_last_rcvd_update deadlock
commit.message:
LU-7593 target: umount vs tgt_last_rcvd_update deadlock

tgt_client_del() and
ofd_commitrw_write->tgt_last_rcvd_update
take transaction and ted->ted_lcd_lock
in different order:

thread1:
    osd_trans_start
    tgt_client_data_update
    tgt_client_del       <<< mutex_lock(&ted->ted_lcd_lock);
    ofd_obd_disconnect
    class_disconnect_export_list
    class_disconnect_exports
    class_cleanup
    ...
    sys_umount

thread2:
    __mutex_lock_slowpath
    mutex_lock          <<< mutex_lock(&ted->ted_lcd_lock);
    tgt_last_rcvd_update
    tgt_txn_stop_cb
    dt_txn_hook_stop
    osd_trans_stop
    ofd_trans_stop
    ofd_commitrw_write
    ...
    tgt_brw_write

Lock only around tgt_client_data_write() inside
the tgt_client_data_update()

Change-Id: Id3f60636be2abb3b70a99ee44b735aab7dfb7657
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-3109
Signed-off-by: Andriy Skulysh <andriy.skulysh@seagate.com>
